# State file

<!-- #F id:b6c7d8ef state_file.location state_file.structure state_file.auto_generated -->

The `.filament` file stores the state of a filament workspace. It lives in the same directory as the spec XML file and is auto-generated by filament commands.

## Structure

The state file contains three required sections and one optional section:

```
# .filament — auto-generated by filament. Do not edit.

[spec]
tool.name=<hash>
tool.binary=<hash>
...

[site]
a1b2c3d4=<hash>
e5f6g7h8=<hash>
...

[state]
a1b2c3d4:tool.name=<hash>
a1b2c3d4:tool.binary=<hash>
e5f6g7h8:public_api.check=<hash>
...

[exclude]
testdata/
*.tmp
```

## Sections

### [spec]

<!-- #F id:9y4im8hg state_file.sections.spec -->

Key: clause id. Value: current spec hash (SHA-256, 64 lowercase hex characters).

Stores the current hash of each clause in the spec. Refreshed by `filament sync`. Used to detect spec drift: if the spec changes, the `[spec]` hashes change, and any `[state]` entry that doesn't match is flagged.

### [site]

<!-- #F id:umc3hd8k state_file.sections.site -->

Key: marker id. Value: content hash.

Stores the hash of the content near each marker in the workspace. The content hash is computed from the N lines immediately after the marker line (default N=10). Refreshed by `filament resolve --site`. Used to detect site drift: if the content near a marker changes, the `[site]` hash changes.

### [state]

<!-- #F id:uoi9l4yh state_file.sections.state -->

Key: `marker_id:clause_id`. Value: reviewed spec hash.

Stores the spec hash that was in effect when the marker was last reviewed against each clause it references. Used to detect spec drift: if the current spec hash for a clause doesn't match the reviewed spec hash, the spec changed since the site was last reviewed.

Each marker-clause pair is tracked independently. Clearing spec drift for one marker does not affect other markers referencing the same clause.

### [exclude]

<!-- #F id:e4xc0001 state_file.exclude -->

Optional. Each entry is a path or glob pattern relative to the state file's directory. Files and directories matching an exclude pattern are skipped during file walks — they are not scanned for markers, and markers in excluded files do not trigger drift findings.

Use the exclude section to exclude test fixtures that contain marker-like patterns for testing purposes, or any other files that should not be tracked by filament.

```
[exclude]
testdata/
*.generated.go
```

The exclude section is preserved across `sync` operations.

## Location

The state file must be in the same directory as the spec XML file. Filament discovers it automatically via `LockFilePath(specPath)` — the directory containing the spec.

## Auto-generated

The state file is auto-generated by filament. Do not edit it manually. The tool overwrites it on every `init`, `resolve`, `sync`, and `migrate` operation.

## Content hash computation

<!-- #F id:pnubihhq marker_format.content_window -->

The content hash is SHA-256 of the normalized content window:

1. Take the 10 lines immediately after the marker line
2. Trim trailing whitespace from each line
3. Normalize line endings to `\n`
4. Collapse consecutive blank lines to a single blank line
5. Hash the result with SHA-256
6. Hex-encode as 64 lowercase characters

If fewer than N lines remain after the marker, all remaining lines are hashed. If the marker is the last line, the hash is computed from an empty string.
